result:
Clean build, no errors.

**Root cause:** `repeatstat` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3845` had a `TODO(port)` stub: when the scope block inside `repeat...until` captured upvalues, the codegen omitted the per-iteration `OP_CLOSE` and the bracketing jumps the C parser emits. As a result, closures created in successive iterations shared the same upvalue slot instead of getting fresh per-iteration captures — so the test's `local x = i` was the same `x` across iterations, breaking the `a[1]() == 1 and a[3]() == 3` assertion at closure.lua:185.

**Fix:** Implemented the missing branch (mirroring `lparser.c:1500-1506`): emit an exit jump, patch `condexit` to the next pc, emit `OP_CLOSE reg_level(fs, bl2.nactvar)`, emit a new jump (becomes the replacement `condexit`), and patch the exit jump to here.

**New state:** Test now passes line 185 and progresses ~75 lines further (next failure at closure.lua:259 — a separate unrelated bug). No temporary instrumentation was added; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green.
